attractions.name: Belcourt of Newport (Official Website)
attractions.description: Belcourt of Newport is a 50,000-square-foot Gilded Age summer villa and stable designed by Richard Morris Hunt for Oliver Hazard Perry Belmont. It is uniquely modeled after the hunting lodge of Versailles and was designed primarily to accommodate Belmont's prize collection of horses and carriages. Recently restored by Carolyn Rafaelian, the mansion now serves as a museum and heritage site that explores Newport’s rich history and architectural legacy (Official Website).
attractions.hours: Friday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Saturday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Sunday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Monday–Thursday: Closed (Tripadvisor, Official Website)

Armenia: An immersive gallery showcasing Armenian life and culture through photography and film (Museum)', "Candlelight Ghost Tours: Evening walking tours that delve into the mansion's fascinating paranormal history (Tours)", 'Extensive Restoration: A multi-year project honored with the Rhody Award for Historic Preservation (Restoration)']
attractions.tips: Tickets should be purchased in advance via the official 3Common ticketing platform as tours are guided and occur on the hour. Be aware that the mansion has limited parking and no on-site snack or water services (Yelp, Official Website).

attractions.name: Chateau-sur-Mer
attractions.description: Chateau-sur-Mer is one of America's greatest Victorian houses and a National Historic Landmark that ushered in the Gilded Age of Newport. Built in 1852 for China trade merchant William Shepard Wetmore, it features palatial Italianate architecture and richly crafted rooms showcasing intricate woodwork, rare wallpapers, and Egyptian and Japanese Revival stencils. Official Site

attractions.name: Chepstow
attractions.description: Chepstow is an elegant Italianate-style villa built in 1860 as the summer residence of Edmund Schermerhorn and later acquired by the Morris family. The mansion is highly evocative of the taste and collections of a founding family descendant, showcasing original Morris-Gallatin furnishings alongside 19th-century American paintings and documents (Preservation Society of Newport County).

attractions.tips: Advance timed tickets are required for all tours as capacity is limited to 10 guests. attractions.name: International Tennis Hall of Fame
attractions.description: The International Tennis Hall of Fame is a premier museum and sports venue located within the historic Newport Casino, a National Historic Landmark. It preserves and celebrates the history of tennis and its greatest champions through a vast collection of memorabilia, interactive exhibits, and the enshrinement of legendary figures. The complex also features the only competition grass courts in the United States that are open for public play. International Tennis Hall of Fame, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Kingscote
attractions.category: Historic House Museum, National Historic Landmark Preservation Society of Newport County
attractions.description: Kingscote is a landmark of the Gothic Revival style in American architecture and one of the first summer 'cottages' built in Newport. Built in 1841 for Southern planter George Noble Jones, it features an irregular roofline, gables, and elaborate Gothic detailing, and remains preserved with original family collections and furnishings. Preservation Society of Newport County, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Marble House
attractions.description: Built between 1888 and 1892 for Mr. and Mrs. William K. Vanderbilt, Marble House is a social and architectural landmark that set the pace for Newport's transformation into a resort of opulent stone palaces. Designed by architect Richard Morris Hunt and inspired by the Petit Trianon at Versailles, the mansion features over 500,000 cubic feet of marble and was a 39th birthday gift for Alva Vanderbilt. Newport Mansions

It is housed in the historic John N.A. Griswold House, a National Historic Landmark and a premier example of American Stick Style architecture. Newport Art Museum

attractions.name: Newport Cliff Walk Official Website
attractions.description: The Newport Cliff Walk is a world-famous 3.5-mile public access walkway along the eastern shore of Aquidneck Island that combines natural seaside beauty with the architectural history of the Gilded Age. Designated as a National Recreation Trail, it winds past iconic mansions like The Breakers and Marble House, offering stunning views of the Atlantic Ocean and Narragansett Bay Official Website, Wikipedia.
attractions.address: 119 Memorial Blvd, Newport, RI 02840 Official Website
attractions.hours: Sunrise to Sunset, Daily Official Website
attractions.days_open: Daily, 365 Days a Year Official Website
attractions.admission: Free Official Website
highlights: 'Designated as a National Recreation Trail, the first of its kind in New England [Official Website', 'Unparalleled views of historic Gilded Age mansions including The Breakers, Marble House, and Rosecliff Wikipedia', 'The iconic "Forty Steps" landmark located at the end of Narragansett Avenue Official Website', 'Varied terrain ranging from smoothly paved paths to rugged rock scrambling in the southern sections Official Website', 'Breathtaking panoramic views of the Rhode Island Sound and the Atlantic Ocean Official Website', 'Public shoreline access preserved since the colonial era Wikipedia']
attractions.tips: Sturdy, closed-toe shoes are essential for the southern half of the walk where the trail becomes rocky. Be vigilant for poison ivy along the path and always check for current detours or closures due to ongoing shoreline maintenance Official Website. Visitors with limited time should focus on the paved segment between Narragansett Avenue and Ruggles Avenue for the best mansion views Official Website.

attractions.name: Rosecliff (Newport Mansions)
attractions.description: Rosecliff is a Gilded Age masterpiece modeled after the Grand Trianon at Versailles, featuring Newport's largest ballroom and a celebrated heart-shaped grand staircase. This elegant mansion overlooking the Atlantic Ocean is renowned for hosting extravagant parties and serving as a film location for movies such as "The Great Gatsby" and "True Lies" (Newport Mansions).

attractions.name: The Breakers Newport Mansions
attractions.description: The Breakers is the grandest of Newport's summer 'cottages' and a symbol of the Vanderbilt family's social and financial pre-eminence in the Gilded Age. Commissioned by Cornelius Vanderbilt II and designed by architect Richard Morris Hunt, the 70-room Italian Renaissance-style palazzo was completed in 1895 and is now a National Historic Landmark. Newport Mansions, Wikipedia

attractions.name: The Elms
attractions.description: Completed in 1901, The Elms was the summer residence of coal tycoon Edward Julius Berwind and his wife, Herminie, modeled after the 18th-century French Château d'Asnières. This Classical Revival mansion was a marvel of its time, featuring the latest technological innovations and serving as a grand stage for Gilded Age society. Today, it is a National Historic Landmark celebrated for its opulent interiors, significant art collections, and meticulously designed sunken gardens. Official Site

attractions.description: The Newport Mansions offer an immersive journey through 250 years of American history, architecture, art, and landscape design. Managed by The Preservation Society of Newport County, these historic house museums, such as The Breakers and Marble House, exemplify the opulent "summer cottages" of the Gilded Age elite. Visitors can explore grand interiors and sprawling gardens that reflect the height of American social and architectural achievement (Newport Mansions About Us).

attractions.name: Bannister's Wharf
attractions.description: Bannister's Wharf is a historic waterfront district in Newport, Rhode Island, originally established in 1742 as a commercial and social lifeline for the colonial town. Today, it serves as a premier destination for shopping, dining, and maritime activities, featuring a world-class marina and luxury accommodations. The wharf is known for its vibrant nautical atmosphere and iconic establishments that capture the essence of Newport's maritime heritage Bannister's Wharf Official Website, Tripadvisor.

Individual shop and restaurant hours typically range from 10:00 AM to 10:00 PM Bannister's Wharf Official Website, Yelp

attractions.name: Bellevue Avenue Historic District Wikipedia
attractions.description: The Bellevue Avenue Historic District is a 606-acre National Historic Landmark known for its collection of opulent Gilded Age mansions built as summer retreats for America's wealthiest families. Stretching over two miles, the district showcases pioneering architectural designs from the late 19th and early 20th centuries, including works by Richard Morris Hunt and McKim, Mead & White. Today, many of these grand estates are preserved as museums, making it one of Newport's premier tourist destinations Wikipedia, GPSmyCity.

Onboard, passengers can enjoy concessions, a bar, and views from both climate-controlled cabins and outside decks. Discover Newport, Block Island Ferry

attractions.name: Bowen's Wharf
attractions.description: Bowen's Wharf is the historic "Anchor of Newport," a vibrant waterfront destination featuring 18th-century commercial buildings, brick walkways, and granite quays. It serves as a premier hub for world-class shopping, award-winning dining, and various maritime tours and excursions. The area preserves Newport's seafaring heritage while offering modern entertainment and scenic views of the harbor.
attractions.hours: Summer (May 22 – Sep 7): Mon-Sat 10am–9pm, Sun 12pm–6pm; Winter (Sep 2 – May 21): Mon-Sat 10am–5pm, Sun 12pm–5pm; Holiday (Nov 28 – Dec 23): Fri-Sat 10am–8pm, Sun-Thu 10am–5pm. (Bowen's Wharf Contact)
attractions.admission: Free (Bowen's Wharf), individual tours and attractions have separate fees.
highlights: ['Historic 18th-century commercial buildings and cobblestone walkways', 'Over 30 unique boutiques, galleries, and specialty shops', 'Award-winning waterfront dining, including the iconic Black Pearl', 'Full-service deep-water marina and working waterfront views', 'Seasonal festivals including the Newport Oyster & Chowder Festival and Christmas Tree Lighting', 'Launch point for harbor cruises, sailing adventures, and fishing charters']
attractions.tips: Visit at sunset for the best views and harbor atmosphere. Parking can be challenging in downtown Newport, so consider using the local parking garages or arriving early. Bring a light jacket even in summer as the ocean breeze can be cool. Check the Pilot House for tour tickets and local information. (Tripadvisor, Bowen's Wharf)

attractions.name: Broadway Newport (Broadway District)
attractions.description: The Broadway District in Newport is a charming, indie neighborhood and local favorite known as 'six blocks of unbridled awesome.' This vibrant area is packed with personality, featuring a mix of craft bars, independent restaurants, and unique boutiques that offer a more authentic experience than typical tourist traps. The site, a former coastal defense battery dating back to the American Revolution, is now a popular destination for sightseeing, scuba diving, and hiking among historic military ruins. Today, it serves as a museum where visitors can explore the complex history of its residents, from prosperous merchant families to the enslaved individuals who lived and worked there.

Measuring 7,350 feet in length, it offers travelers stunning panoramic views of the water, the Plum Beach Lighthouse, and the neighboring Newport Bridge. The bridge's clean architectural lines and elevation make it a prominent landmark in the Rhode Island coastal landscape.

attractions.description: The Newport Colony House is the fourth oldest statehouse still standing in the United States, completed in 1741 to house Rhode Island's colonial government. Today, it is a National Historic Landmark managed as a museum that preserves its original interior arrangement and architectural significance (Newport Historical Society, Wikipedia)

It preserves the largest contiguous open space on Aquidneck Island, featuring seven miles of hiking trails through diverse habitats such as woodlands, hay fields, and salt marshes. The sanctuary offers panoramic views of the Atlantic Ocean and serves as a vital haven for more than 250 species of birds (Norman Bird Sanctuary, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Redwood Library and Athenaeum Official Site
attractions.description: Founded in 1747, the Redwood Library and Athenaeum is America's oldest purpose-built library and the oldest continuously operating lending library in its original location. It serves as a subscription library, museum, and research center, housed in the nation's earliest public Neoclassical building designed by Peter Harrison. The institution holds over 200,000 volumes, including a significant portion of its original 1747 collection, and an exceptional collection of 18th and 19th-century portraits. Official Site, Wikipedia

attractions.tips: The main entrance is located on Redwood Street rather than Bellevue Avenue. A small free parking lot is available for visitors directly across from the Redwood Street entrance. Fully restored to its early 20th-century appearance, it serves as a living museum and environmental education center where visitors can experience maritime history and explore a surrounding wildlife refuge. The site offers unique off-grid overnight stays, allowing guests to 'walk in the steps of a lighthouse keeper' while enjoying panoramic ocean views. (Rose Island Official Site, Travel + Leisure)

attractions.tips: Visitors must arrive by ferry or private small craft; no anchoring at the dock is permitted. attractions.name: Washington Square (Newport, Rhode Island)
attractions.description: Washington Square is the historical and geographical heart of Newport, serving as a trapezoidal civic space since the city's settlement in 1639. Flanked by iconic colonial buildings like the Old Colony House and the Brick Market, it features Eisenhower Park with its paved pathways, historic statues, and a recreated 19th-century horse trough fountain. Today, it remains a vibrant hub for community gatherings, historic tours, and local commerce within the Newport Historic District.
attractions.address: Washington Square (also includes Eisenhower Park), Newport, RI 02840, USA
attractions.hours: 6:00 AM – 1:00 AM Daily (Park hours; the square is a public thoroughfare accessible 24/7) (Airial Travel)
attractions.admission: Free (Public Square/Park) (Airial Travel)
highlights: 'Old Colony House (1739) – The fourth oldest extant statehouse in the U.S. ([Newport Historical Society)', 'Brick Market (1762) – Designed by Peter Harrison, now housing the Museum of Newport History (Newport Discovery Guide)', 'Eisenhower Park – A central green space with a statue of Oliver Hazard Perry and a historic bandstand (The Cultural Landscape Foundation)', 'Horse Trough Fountain – A recreated 19th-century bronze fountain at the center of the square (Wikipedia))', 'Newport Historic District – Part of a 250-acre National Historic Landmark area (Newport Discovery Guide)']
attractions.tips: The square is a great starting point for historical walking tours. Visit on Saturdays during summer to see if the Old Colony House is open for tours. Don't miss the Museum of Newport History located in the Brick Market at the western foot of the square. (Newport History Tours, Newport Discovery Guide)
attractions.nearby: Museum of Newport History, Touro Synagogue, Jane Pickens Theater, Long Wharf, and the White Horse Tavern (Newport Discovery Guide)

attractions.name: Wharf Fishhouse Coastal Seafood & Tavern (formerly Wharf Pub)
attractions.description: Wharf Fishhouse Coastal Seafood & Tavern, formerly known as Wharf Southern Kitchen & Whiskey Bar and The Wharf Pub, is a waterfront dining staple in historic Bowen's Wharf. It offers a classic New England experience with fresh seafood, craft cocktails, and an extensive collection of bourbons and whiskeys. Guests can enjoy harbor views and a lively atmosphere in the heart of Newport's maritime district.

attractions.tips: Reservations are recommended for window seating to get the best views of passing sailboats. The bar team is known for personal and patient whiskey recommendations for those looking to explore their extensive list. Outdoor seating is a must-visit during sunny days to soak up the waterfront buzz (Newport 744).

Known for its hand-selected USDA Prime steaks and fresh New England seafood, it offers both refined indoor dining and al fresco experiences at its open-air Portside bar. Official Site, AAA Trip Canvas

attractions.name: Diego's Newport
attractions.description: Diego’s Newport is a waterfront Mexican fusion restaurant and craft cocktail bar located in the heart of historic Bowen’s Wharf. The establishment features scratch-made dishes such as creative tacos and burritos alongside a laid-back, punk-rock vibe and seasonal outdoor seating with harbor views. Known for its extensive agave spirit selection and daily brunch, it serves as a year-round destination for both locals and visitors.

If you're looking for a quieter experience, visit during the week to enjoy 'Taco Tuesday' or their off-season specials. Be sure to check out the second-floor bar for the best views of the Newport harbor.

attractions.name: Fluke Newport (formerly Fluke Wine Bar & Kitchen)
attractions.description: Fluke Newport is a refined, seafood-forward restaurant and cocktail bar located in the heart of historic Bowen’s Wharf. Known for its sophisticated yet relaxed atmosphere, it offers modern American cuisine emphasizing fresh, locally caught Rhode Island seafood and a world-class wine selection. The venue features two levels of dining with an intimate third-floor bar that provides panoramic sunset views of Newport Harbor.

attractions.name: The Clarke Cooke House
attractions.description: The Clarke Cooke House is a legendary Newport landmark and restaurant located on Bannister's Wharf, housed in a historic colonial structure built in 1780. Renowned as the 'heartbeat of Newport,' it offers an unparalleled atmosphere with multiple dining levels ranging from casual bistro fare to sophisticated fine dining on the Porch. It has long been a favorite destination for royalty, yachting stars, and celebrities, serving innovative Mediterranean-influenced American cuisine. Clarke Cooke House, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Kitchen: Sun-Thurs 5:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 5:00 PM - 11:00 PM; Lunch: Fri-Sun 11:30 AM - 4:00 PM; Brunch: Sun 11:00 AM - 3:00 PM; Bar: Nightly from 4:00 PM Clarke Cooke House
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant) Clarke Cooke House

attractions.name: Brenton Point State Park
attractions.description: Occupying the former grounds of one of Newport's grandest estates, Brenton Point State Park affords its visitors one of the most commanding and spectacular views on the East Coast. The 89-acre site is ideal for picnicking, hiking, bird watching, fishing, or simply enjoying the cool breezes and majestic vistas where Narragansett Bay meets the Atlantic Ocean. (Rhode Island State Parks)
attractions.hours: Monday: Sunrise to Sunset; Tuesday: Sunrise to Sunset; Wednesday: Sunrise to Sunset; Thursday: Sunrise to Sunset; Friday: Sunrise to Sunset; Saturday: Sunrise to Sunset; Sunday: Sunrise to Sunset (Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (Rhode Island State Parks)
highlights: 'Commanding views of the Atlantic Ocean and Narragansett Bay ([Rhode Island State Parks)', 'The Portuguese Discovery Monument, a tribute to maritime navigators (Rhode Island State Parks)', 'Excellent conditions for kite flying, hosting the annual Newport Kite Festival (Visit Rhode Island)', 'Scenic walking and hiking trails through lush coastal landscapes (Rhode Island State Parks)', 'Prime locations for saltwater fishing and photography (Rhode Island State Parks)', 'Extensive picnicking areas with cool ocean breezes (Rhode Island State Parks)']
attractions.tips: The park is a 'carry-in/carry-out' facility, so be prepared to take all trash with you. Restrooms are typically open only from May 1 to October 31. It is often very windy, so bringing a kite or an extra layer of clothing is recommended even on sunny days. (Rhode Island State Parks)

attractions.name: Fort Adams State Park
attractions.description: Fort Adams State Park is a 200-acre historic site and public recreation area preserving a massive coastal fortification that was active from the mid-19th to mid-20th centuries. It is renowned for its complex military architecture and as the long-time home of the Newport Jazz and Folk Festivals (Rhode Island State Parks, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Tours: 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM daily (seasonal); Park grounds: 6:00 AM – 9:00 PM or sunrise to sunset (Fort Adams Trust, Tripadvisor)
attractions.days_open: 7 days a week (tours seasonal from April to October) (Fort Adams Trust)
attractions.admission: Guided Tours: $15+ per person; Self-Guided Admission: $10+ per person; Park Vehicle Entry: $10 for RI residents, $20 for non-residents; Free for pedestrians and cyclists (Lake.com, Fort Adams Trust)
highlights: ['Historic Fort Adams (National Historic Landmark)', 'Newport Jazz and Folk Festivals venue', 'Panoramic views of Newport Harbor and Narragansett Bay', "The Eisenhower House (former 'Summer White House')", 'Sail Newport and various boating activities', 'Kirwin Memorial Rugby Pitch and athletic fields']
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes for the expansive grounds, check seasonal tour schedules in advance, and visit the 'Bay Walk' for the best sunset photography spots (Flood Mazda Blog, Discover Newport).

attractions.name: Newport Country Club
attractions.description: Newport Country Club is a historic private golf club founded in 1893 that played a foundational role in American golf history. It hosted both the first U.S. Amateur Championship and the first U.S. Open in 1895 and is one of the five founding member clubs of the United States Golf Association (USGA). Wikipedia, Newport Country Club
attractions.hours: Clubhouse and locker rooms typically open 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM (unless otherwise posted). Newport Country Club Rules
attractions.admission: Private; access is by member sponsorship only. Newport Country Club
highlights: 'Hosted the first U.S. Amateur and U.S. Open championships in 1895. [Wikipedia', 'One of the five founding members of the United States Golf Association (USGA). Newport Country Club', 'Features an iconic Beaux-Arts clubhouse designed by Whitney Warren. Golfers Journal', "Site of Tiger Woods' 1995 U.S. Amateur victory. Newport Country Club", 'Historic 18-hole course with significant designs by William Davis and A.W. Tillinghast. Newport Country Club']
attractions.tips: The club is strictly private; golfers must be sponsored and accompanied by a member. A strict dress code is enforced, requiring collared shirts and slacks or Bermuda shorts; blue jeans and metal spikes are prohibited. Cell phone use is restricted to motor vehicles. attractions.name: Sachuest Point National Wildlife Refuge
attractions.description: Sachuest Point National Wildlife Refuge is a 242-acre coastal sanctuary located in Middletown, Rhode Island, renowned for its dramatic rocky shorelines and diverse bird populations. The refuge serves as a vital wintering ground for migratory birds, most notably hosting the largest wintering population of Harlequin Ducks on the East Coast. Visitors can enjoy over 2.5 miles of scenic trails that offer panoramic views of the Atlantic Ocean and Sachuest Bay, along with opportunities for wildlife photography and seasonal saltwater fishing (U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service, Discover Newport).
attractions.hours: Trails: Sunrise to Sunset; Visitor Center: 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM daily (U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service)
attractions.admission: Free (U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service, WBSM)
highlights: 'Scenic coastal trails including the 1.5-mile Ocean View Loop and the 1.1-mile Flint Point Loop ([U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service)', 'Birdwatching opportunities for Harlequin Ducks, Snowy Owls, and various raptors (U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service)', 'Visitor Center featuring educational exhibits, a gift shop, and seasonal programs (U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service)', 'Dramatic rocky shorelines with tide pools and panoramic ocean vistas (Airial Travel)', 'Saltwater fishing access from the shore (permit required) (U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service)']
attractions.tips: Pets and bicycles are strictly prohibited on the refuge trails to protect local wildlife. Visitors should be mindful of ticks, especially in warmer months, and bring sun protection as there is very little shade along the coastal paths (U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service, Airial Travel).
